name: TDI Mattermost Policy Plugin
description: Enforces external policy decisions via TDI for message and channel access control

settings_schema.header: Configure the Mattermost Policy Plugin to integrate with TDI for external policy enforcement.
settings_schema.footer: For more information, see the plugin documentation.

settings.help_text: Base URL for the policy service (e.g., https://tdi.example.com)
Policy namespace used when building endpoint paths
API key for authenticating with TDI (optional)
When enabled, all messages will be checked against the policy before posting
When enabled, users joining channels will be checked against the policy
When enabled, message edits will be checked against the policy
When enabled, message deletions are reported to TDI for audit. Mattermost does not support blocking deletions.
When enabled, file uploads will be checked against the policy
When enabled, user logins will be checked against the policy
When enabled, new channels will be auto-classified and checked against policy
When enabled, emoji reactions will be checked against the policy
When enabled, new user creation is reported to TDI for audit or onboarding
When enabled, users joining teams will be checked against the policy
When enabled, users leaving teams are reported to TDI for audit
When enabled, users leaving channels are reported to TDI for audit
When enabled, newly posted messages are reported to TDI for audit
When enabled, message edits are reported to TDI for audit
When enabled, successful logins are reported to TDI for audit
When enabled, messages are reported to TDI before reaching the client (audit; requires server 9.3+)
When enabled, user deactivations are reported to TDI for audit
When enabled, push notifications are checked against policy before sending
When enabled, server config changes are validated via TDI before saving
When enabled, SAML logins are reported to TDI for audit (requires server 10.7+)
Maximum time to wait for policy decision before denying (fail-secure)
Maximum uploaded file size that the plugin will spool, hash, and send to TDI file policy. Files larger than this are denied when file upload policy is enabled. Use 0 for the default 100 MiB.
Enable detailed logging for troubleshooting (may impact performance)
When enabled, system administrators bypass all policy checks
API token for Mattermost REST API (e.g. Personal Access Token). Required for channel classification via access control policies. Used to search and assign policies.

Map policy attribute names to Mattermost/LDAP fields. Supports: built-in (first_name, last_name, nickname, position), user.Props keys, or LDAP attribute names for LDAP users. Custom profile attributes (SecurityClearance, Nationality, etc.) from Enterprise System properties are always included by field name.
